_doc: Per-file WPT expectations for nub's Worker conformance gate. An entry is one of: {} (every subtest must pass), {skip:<reason>} (file not run — needs a DOM substrate nub has no business providing), or {fail:{expected:[<subtest names>], note:<why>}} (those subtests are documented, accepted divergences; every OTHER subtest in the file must still pass). expected-fail entries are DOCUMENTED divergences, not silent gaps — most are inherited from node:worker_threads and shared with native Node. The Blob-instanceof divergence (D2) is a real nub finding pending a maintainer document-vs-fix decision; see the note. An unexpected pass on an expected-fail subtest is reported (so the list can be tightened) but does not fail the run; an unexpected FAIL (regression) does.

webmessaging/MessageEvent-trusted.any.js.skip: Both subtests assert MessageEvent.isTrusted===true. Node's worker_threads MessageEvent is isTrusted=false (verified: native Node is also false), so this is a Node-inherited divergence, not nub-specific. The BroadcastChannel subtest also needs an on-disk worker support file we do not vendor.

fail.note: PORT AUTO-START (Node-inherited). The spec requires that addEventListener('message') WITHOUT start() must not deliver; nub follows node:worker_threads, which starts a port on any listener (workerd shares this). Documented in the worker FAQ. The file itself notes it duplicates message-channels/no-start.any.js.

expected: Test Description: Messages can contain Blobs.
fail.note: DOM gap: the test reads the cloned Blob with `FileReader`, which is not a Node global (no DOM substrate). The Blob itself clones correctly (instanceof + bytes are fixed); only the FileReader-based readback is unavailable. Out of scope, like the other DOM-only rows.

PORT AUTO-START (Node-inherited). addEventListener('message') without start() should not deliver per spec; node:worker_threads (and workerd) start the port on any listener. Documented in the worker FAQ. Same divergence as MessagePort_initial_disabled.any.js.

webmessaging/message-channels/worker-pos.skip: CLOSE() SEMANTICS divergence. These probe the browser's GRACEFUL self.close() — queued tasks (and a postMessage issued right after close()) still run/flush. nub's worker-scope close() is process.exit(0) (hard), so a postMessage after close() is lost and the test's async_test never completes (0 subtests). A documentable divergence; skipped because it cannot yield a meaningful pass/fail under a hard close.

webmessaging/message-channels/worker.any.skip: CLOSE() SEMANTICS divergence (same as worker-post-after-close): the blob: worker calls self.close() then postMessage(true); nub's hard process.exit(0) close drops the post, so the async_test never completes.

expected: Array ImageData object, ImageData 1x1 transparent black
Array ImageData object, ImageData 1x1 non-transparent non-black
Object ImageData object, ImageData 1x1 transparent black
Object ImageData object, ImageData 1x1 non-transparent non-black
Array ImageBitmap object, ImageBitmap 1x1 transparent black
Array ImageBitmap object, ImageBitmap 1x1 transparent non-black
Object ImageBitmap object, ImageBitmap 1x1 transparent black
Object ImageBitmap object, ImageBitmap 1x1 transparent non-black
A subclass instance will deserialize as its closest serializable superclass
versioned.note: NODE structured-clone limitation on every line BELOW 24: structuredClone/postMessage of a File deserializes to a plain Blob — File-ness is lost. Verified on vanilla Node (no nub) on 18.19, 20, and 22 (22.15.1/22.16.0): `structuredClone(new File([…])) instanceof File === false`. Node 24+ fixed it (round-trips as File). nub is faithful here — the loss is in the underlying node:worker_threads/v8 serializer, which nub does not intermediate for a native MessageChannel; nub backfills the `File` global on the 18.x floor (runtime/polyfills.cjs) so the test can construct the File, but cannot make the native serializer preserve its subclass identity. The 'subclass deserializes as closest serializable superclass' subtest fails for the same reason (the File subclass collapses to Blob).

expected: A detached ArrayBuffer cannot be transferred
Transferring a non-transferable platform object fails
Serializing a non-serializable platform object fails
versioned.note: NODE native-serializer error behavior on lines BELOW 22: Node's native MessageChannel postMessage does NOT reject these transfer/serialize error cases on Node 18/20 — re-transferring an already-detached ArrayBuffer, transferring a non-transferable Blob, and serializing a non-serializable Response all silently succeed instead of throwing DataCloneError. Verified on vanilla Node (no nub) on 18.19 and 20 (no throw) vs Node 22.15+/24 (throws DataCloneError). nub does not intermediate a native MessageChannel's serializer, so this is the underlying Node behavior, not a nub divergence — expected-fail on the 18/20 legs only. The throw boundary is verified at the matrix legs (no-throw on 18.19/20, throws on 22.15+); if a sub-22.15 leg (22.0-22.14) is ever added, re-verify the exact minor where the native rejection landed.

NODE 18 V8 limitation: resizable/growable ArrayBuffer (the `maxByteLength` option + `ArrayBuffer.prototype.resize`) is a V8 feature that landed in Node 20 — it is absent on the 18.x compat floor (`new ArrayBuffer(n, {maxByteLength}).resize` is not a function). nub cannot add a V8 engine feature, so these resizable-buffer serialize/transfer subtests are expected-fail on the Node 18 leg only; Node 20+ has the feature and passes them.

fail.note: Structured-clone battery (152 subtests). The version-independent expected fails are all NON-Blob: (1) DOM-only types with NO Node substrate — FileList/ImageData/ImageBitmap need `document`/canvas (throw 'document is not defined' / 'OffscreenCanvas is not defined'); legitimately out of scope (no DOM on Node), kept expected-fail rather than skip so a future DOM polyfill surfaces as an unexpected-pass. (2) 'Growable SharedArrayBuffer instanceof ArrayBuffer' — a growable SAB is not an ArrayBuffer instance under Node. Plus three VERSION-GATED bands (see `versioned`), all genuine below-floor Node limitations nub cannot fix and does not mask on the versions where the behavior is correct: File→Blob clone loss on Node <24, native-serializer non-rejection on Node <22, and resizable-ArrayBuffer absence on Node 18. The 19 Blob clone rows now PASS — `cloned instanceof Blob` AND the Blob's bytes-via-Response are fixed (runtime/worker-blob-url.cjs makes globalThis.Blob a Proxy over the native Blob, keeping `globalThis.Blob.prototype === node:buffer.Blob.prototype` so a deserialized native Blob passes both `instanceof` and undici's webidl brand check). Every Map/Set/Date/typed-array/SAB-identity/transfer/circular case also passes.
